page.title: Celebrate Andalusia Day on a Yacht in Marbella

data.meta_description: Celebrate Andalusia Day on a luxury yacht charter in Marbella. Explore the Costa del Sol from Puerto Banús with a skipper, drinks & snacks included. Book now.
data.summary: A guide to celebrating Andalusia Day (28 February) with a yacht charter in Marbella. Includes fleet options, pricing, and tips for enjoying the holiday from the water.
key_facts: Andalusia Day is 28 February, a public holiday in Andalusia.
Charters depart from Puerto Banús, Marbella, with a licensed skipper.
All charters include fuel, drinks (water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava), and snacks.
Prices from €749 for 2 hours on an Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39 (up to 11 pax).
Larger yachts like the Mangusta 80 (24m) seat 12 and include a free jet ski from €4,719 for 4 hours.
Book via WhatsApp for custom durations and boats not listed with fixed rates.
Nearby anchorages: Cabopino, Cala del Faro, and Estepona coast.
Spanish IVA (21%) is included in all prices – no hidden extras.

faq.q: What is Andalusia Day and when is it celebrated?
faq.a: Andalusia Day (Día de Andalucía) is the annual public holiday on 28 February, commemorating the 1980 autonomy referendum. It is marked with parades, regional flags, and traditional food across the Costa del Sol.

faq.q: Can I charter a yacht on Andalusia Day in Marbella?
faq.a: Yes, our fleet is available on 28 February. All charters depart from Puerto Banús with a licensed skipper, fuel, drinks, and snacks included. Book early as demand is high for the holiday.

faq.q: What is the best yacht for a group celebration on Andalusia Day?
faq.a: For groups up to 12, the Mangusta 80 (24m) offers ample deck space and a free jet ski. The Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m) is ideal for 12 with a spacious flybridge. For smaller groups, the Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) is a great value option.

faq.q: Are drinks and food included in the charter price?
faq.a: Yes, every charter includes water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, and light snacks. You are welcome to bring your own food, but all beverages are provided by the crew.

faq.q: Where can we anchor during an Andalusia Day yacht trip?
faq.a: Popular anchorages near Marbella include Cabopino, Cala del Faro, and the coast off Estepona. These spots offer calm waters and scenic views of La Concha mountain and the Gibraltar Strait.

faq.q: How do I book a yacht for Andalusia Day?
faq.a: You can book directly via WhatsApp on our website. For boats with fixed pricing (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39, Mangusta 80 variants), you can reserve with a deposit. All other boats are priced on request.

faq.q: What is the cancellation policy for a charter on Andalusia Day?
faq.a: Cancellations made more than 14 days before the charter are fully refundable. Within 14 days, a 50% charge applies. No refunds for cancellations within 48 hours. Weather-related cancellations are handled case by case.

faq.q: Is it possible to watch the Andalusia Day fireworks from a yacht?
faq.a: Fireworks are not typical for this holiday, but you can enjoy the coastal views from the water. Evening charters offer a unique perspective of Marbella's Golden Mile lit up after sunset.

data.body_html:
Andalusia Day (28 February) is a public holiday that brings the Costa del Sol to life with regional pride, flamenco, and local festivities. What better way to celebrate than with an Andalusia Day yacht charter Marbella? Escape the crowds and enjoy the Mediterranean from the deck of a luxury motor yacht, with drinks, snacks, and a skipper all included. Here is everything you need to plan your day on the water.

Why charter a yacht for Andalusia Day?

Andalusia Day marks the anniversary of the 1980 autonomy referendum, and towns across the region celebrate with parades, flags, and traditional food. Marbella and Puerto Banús are no exception. A yacht charter lets you enjoy the holiday atmosphere without the packed beaches or busy promenades. You can anchor in a quiet cove, sip cava, and watch the coastline from the water. With the Sierra Blanca and La Concha as a backdrop, it is a memorable way to honour the region.

Our fleet for Andalusia Day charters

All our yachts depart from Puerto Banús and come with a licensed skipper, fuel, insurance, and a selection of drinks and snacks. Here are the top choices for your celebration:

  • Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) – €749 for 2 hours, €2,299 for 8 hours. Great for intimate groups.
  • Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax) – Same pricing as the Astondoa 40. Slightly more space.
  • Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) – From €4,719 for 4 hours, includes a free jet ski. Ideal for larger parties.
  • Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m, 12 pax) – Pricing on request. Spacious flybridge for sunbathing.
  • Mangusta 80 (Grey) and Mangusta 80 (White) – Same specs as above, different colour schemes.

For other boats such as the Ferretti 94 (29m, 12 pax) or Pershing 46 (14m, 10 pax), please enquire via WhatsApp for custom pricing and availability.

What is included in your charter

Every booking includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). There are no hidden costs. You are welcome to bring your own food, but all beverages are provided by the crew. This makes planning simple – just show up at Puerto Banús and enjoy.

Best anchorages for Andalusia Day

From Puerto Banús, you can cruise east or west along the Costa del Sol. Recommended spots include:

  • Cabopino – A sandy cove with calm waters, 20 minutes east of Marbella.
  • Cala del Faro – A rocky inlet near the lighthouse, perfect for swimming.
  • Estepona coast – Quiet anchorages with views of the Gibraltar Strait.
  • Cala Cortés – Small, secluded beach accessible only by boat.

Your skipper will know the best spots based on wind and sea conditions. Expect water temperatures around 15°C in late February – a wetsuit is advisable for swimming.

Pricing overview

BoatDurationPrice (incl. all)
Astondoa 40 / Azimut 392 hours€749
Astondoa 40 / Azimut 398 hours€2,299
Mangusta 80 (all variants)4 hours (min.)€4,719
Other boats (e.g., Ferretti 94, Pershing 46)CustomOn request via WhatsApp

All prices include VAT, skipper, fuel, drinks, and snacks. No surprises.

How to book your Andalusia Day yacht charter

Booking is straightforward. Use WhatsApp to check availability for your preferred date and boat. For fixed-price boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39, Mangusta 80 variants), you can confirm with a deposit. For other yachts, we will provide a quote within hours. We recommend booking at least two weeks in advance, as Andalusia Day is a popular holiday. Browse all yacht charters in Marbella for more options.

Tips for a great Andalusia Day on the water

  • Dress in layers – February mornings can be cool (12°C), but afternoons reach 18°C.
  • Bring sunscreen – the Mediterranean sun is strong even in winter.
  • Arrive 15 minutes early at Puerto Banús for boarding.
  • Consider a half-day charter (4 hours) to combine sailing with on-land festivities.
  • Ask your skipper about local Andalusia Day traditions – they often share great stories.

Frequently asked questions

What is Andalusia Day and when is it celebrated?

Andalusia Day (Día de Andalucía) is the annual public holiday on 28 February, commemorating the 1980 autonomy referendum. It is marked with parades, regional flags, and traditional food across the Costa del Sol.

Can I charter a yacht on Andalusia Day in Marbella?

Yes, our fleet is available on 28 February. All charters depart from Puerto Banús with a licensed skipper, fuel, drinks, and snacks included. Book early as demand is high for the holiday.

What is the best yacht for a group celebration on Andalusia Day?

For groups up to 12, the Mangusta 80 (24m) offers ample deck space and a free jet ski. The Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m) is ideal for 12 with a spacious flybridge. For smaller groups, the Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) is a great value option.

Are drinks and food included in the charter price?

Yes, every charter includes water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, and light snacks. You are welcome to bring your own food, but all beverages are provided by the crew.

Where can we anchor during an Andalusia Day yacht trip?

Popular anchorages near Marbella include Cabopino, Cala del Faro, and the coast off Estepona. These spots offer calm waters and scenic views of La Concha mountain and the Gibraltar Strait.

How do I book a yacht for Andalusia Day?

You can book directly via WhatsApp on our website. For boats with fixed pricing (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39, Mangusta 80 variants), you can reserve with a deposit. All other boats are priced on request.

What is the cancellation policy for a charter on Andalusia Day?

Cancellations made more than 14 days before the charter are fully refundable. Within 14 days, a 50% charge applies. No refunds for cancellations within 48 hours. Weather-related cancellations are handled case by case.

Is it possible to watch the Andalusia Day fireworks from a yacht?

Fireworks are not typical for this holiday, but you can enjoy the coastal views from the water. Evening charters offer a unique perspective of Marbella's Golden Mile lit up after sunset.

Make this Andalusia Day unforgettable with a yacht charter in Marbella. Whether you choose the intimate Astondoa 40 or the spacious Mangusta 80, the Costa del Sol is yours to explore. Check availability for your date and let the crew take care of the rest.
